steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: The feature to display is $c$, the factor by which $f(x)$ multiplies for every increase of 2 in $x$. Compute $c$: from any form, $c = f(x+2)/f(x)$. Using the displayed form as the reference, $c = 2^{6 \cdot 2} = 2^{12} = 4{,}096$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Rewrite the growth factor so an increase of $2$ in $x$ corresponds to one full power of the base: $2^{6x}=\left(2^{12}\right)^{x/2}=4096^{x/2}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Scan each choice for 4,096 appearing literally as the base: A has base 2, B has base 8, and C has base 64. Only D writes the base as $4{,}096$ with exponent $x/2$, so a step of 2 in $x$ raises the base to the first power exactly once.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content: That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
